items.prompt: selects among algebraic, graphical, tabular, parametric, and polar representations in bounded AP Precalculus-style modeling and interpretation tasks
items.expected_evidence: choose and justify an appropriate advanced representation or model family for a bounded AP Precalculus-style task
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner chooses a representation or function family that preserves the structure of the situation and supports a defensible analysis.

items.prompt: explains how parameters, periodicity, symmetry, and representation choices shape AP Precalculus models across trigonometric, parametric, and polar settings
items.expected_evidence: explain how parameters and representations control periodicity, symmetry, growth, and change
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains why the model behaves as it does instead of reciting disconnected procedures for separate representations.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to parametric_polar_disconnect.
items.expected_evidence: compare function behavior across algebraic, graphical, parametric, and polar forms without losing the underlying structure
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies where the represented path, parameter, or feature was interpreted inconsistently across forms.

items.prompt: interprets a less-familiar AP Precalculus modeling context and identifies which representation, family, or feature most strongly governs the situation
items.expected_evidence: connect trigonometric, parametric, polar, and family-synthesis reasoning in one coherent modeling analysis
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves coherent cross-representation reasoning and identifies the feature that actually governs the modeled behavior.
